idea.title: Tiny Markov Dunge
idea.hook: A 200-byte room-generator paints your dungeon in real time — your only weapon is editing the seed before the boss spawns from your own typo.
idea.prototype: 2D top-down canvas. 6-char hex seed at top; 80-byte Markov rule table paints a 12×12 tile dungeon left-to-right as you watch. WASD to move; pause and rewrite one seed char per room cleared (costs 1 ink). Bad seeds cluster enemies / boss tiles next to spawn. Lose when ink=0 AND boss tile spawns adjacent.
idea.why_novel: First factory game where the player can DIRECTLY EDIT the procedural generator at runtime. Existing PCG games hide the seed; this exposes it as the primary input. The Markov chain table is small enough (80B of weights) that single-character edits produce visible structural shifts.
idea.non_timer_pressure: resource scarcity — finite seed-edit ink, depletes per character rewrite
idea.lose_condition: ink counter hits 0 and a boss tile spawns adjacent to player

sources.title: Sub-1MB ML in hypercasual — viable?
sources.snippet: Discussion of small generative models baked into web games — Markov chains, tiny VAEs, parameter budgets <1MB. Whether they produce useful variety per run.

sources.title: Procedural-gen aesthetics post
sources.snippet: Visible procedural generation as a gameplay verb — letting players watch and influence the generator rather than hiding it.

sources.title: Tiny model PCG experiment
sources.snippet: Quantised micro-VAE for level layout, weighted Markov on hand-crafted tile rules. Reference for the 80-byte rule table baked into this game.

sources.title: Tiny-model PCG one-off
sources.snippet: Bake a sub-1MB model into a single game just to see if a tiny generator produces interesting variety per run. Pick smallest sensible genre. Goal is a data point on whether sub-1MB ML in a hypercasual ships at all. (2026-05-14)

synthesizer_brief: Today's digest curator picked this from Tim's IDEAS.md backlog over 2 other novel digest entries that had direct shipped duplicates (shader_polish_sprint, tier1_hook). The IDEAS.md entry asked specifically for 'smallest sensible genre' — chosen format is top-down dungeon crawler with PLAYER-EXPOSED seed editing as the novel verb. Markov-chain rule table is hand-coded (~80 bytes of weights), no actual ML dependency.

alternatives.title: Ice Fracture Foreman
alternatives.hook: Tap a frozen lake to crack it in branching fissures, isolating fish into a shrinking pond before the cracks reach your hut.
alternatives.why_novel: Corral-via-fracture spatial puzzle — mechanically distinct from existing ice games (roller-cleanup, shatter-score, split-line).

alternatives.title: Shader Saturday
alternatives.hook: Drag the post-process slider chain to match the day's screenshot — wrong order makes your render look like AI slop and judges roast you.
alternatives.why_novel: Lives-based meta-game over a shader chain; skipped because direct overlap with shader_polish_sprint.

alternatives.title: Token Throttle
alternatives.hook: You're a dispatcher routing tasks to cheap or expensive AI agents — pick wrong and your monthly budget burns before quota refills.
alternatives.why_novel: Complexity-tagged routing with rework-loop penalty — picked as the 3rd build despite saturated AI-cost theme.

alternatives.title: Tier-1 Pitch
alternatives.hook: Drag elements onto your store page — title, hook, icon, screenshot — and watch English-speaking testers either click Wishlist or bounce in disgust.
alternatives.why_novel: Direct clone of tier1_hook (store-page asset-drag builder shipped 2026-05-14); skipped.
